prerequisites: A Pocket account whose saved list you want to extract.

A registered Pocket application, which provides the platform consumer_key that identifies your app to the Pocket API. Register one at the Pocket developer 'Create a New App' page (getpocket.com/developer/apps/new). Note: an old Read It Later / Pocket apikey will not work; a new consumer key is required.
A user-specific access_token obtained by completing Pocket's OAuth 2.0 variant authorization flow for the account you want to read.

Optionally, the values you want to use to scope the pull: state (unread, archive, all), favorite (0 or 1), tag, content_type (article, video, image), detail_type (simple, complete), sort (newest, oldest, title, site), search, domain, and a since unix timestamp for incremental syncs.

authMethods.name: Consumer key + OAuth access token
authMethods.summary: Pocket's v3 API uses a variant of OAuth 2.0. Every request requires two secrets that map to the connector's secret fields: consumer_key (the platform key identifying your registered application) and access_token (the per-user token authorizing access to a specific Pocket account's data). Both are sent on each call to the /v3/get retrieve endpoint. There is no single API key; the access_token is produced only by completing the request-token, user-authorization, and access-token-conversion steps of the OAuth flow.

setupSteps.title: Register an application to get a consumer key
setupSteps.body: Sign in to Pocket and register your application at the developer 'Create a New App' page (getpocket.com/developer/apps/new). Registration associates your app with a platform consumer_key (format like 1234-abcd1234abcd1234abcd1234) that identifies your app to the API. Provide this value as the connector's consumer_key field.

setupSteps.title: Obtain a request token
setupSteps.body: Make an HTTPS POST to https://getpocket.com/v3/oauth/request with your consumer_key and a redirect_uri. The response returns a request token in the 'code' field. All Pocket auth calls must be POST over HTTPS, with a Content-Type of application/x-www-form-urlencoded or application/json (set charset, e.g. UTF-8), and an X-Accept header for the desired response format.

setupSteps.title: Authorize the request token in Pocket
setupSteps.body: Redirect the user to https://getpocket.com/auth/authorize?request_token=YOUR_REQUEST_TOKEN&redirect_uri=YOUR_REDIRECT_URI. The user signs in (if needed) and approves the app. Pocket then calls your redirect_uri to signal that authorization is complete.

setupSteps.title: Convert the request token into an access token
setupSteps.body: Make an HTTPS POST to https://getpocket.com/v3/oauth/authorize with your consumer_key and the 'code' (request token) from the earlier step. The response returns the access_token (and the username). Provide this value as the connector's access_token field. You can clear test-user tokens at getpocket.com/connected_accounts.

setupSteps.title: Scope the data pull with optional retrieve filters
setupSteps.body: The connector reads from the /v3/get endpoint, which accepts optional filters that map to the config fields. state = unread (default), archive, or all. favorite = 0 (un-favorited) or 1 (favorited). content_type (Pocket contentType) = article, video, or image. detail_type (Pocket detailType) = simple (basic fields) or complete (includes tags, images, authors, videos). sort = newest, oldest, title, or site. tag filters by a tag name (or _untagged_). search returns items whose title or url contain the string. domain returns items from a particular domain. since takes a unix timestamp and returns only items modified since then, which supports incremental syncs. Leave any of these unset to use Pocket's defaults.
